feedback.text: One thing that companies are afraid to do in the digital world is to sell a product, and then to decide, as it seems to him, to dictate it. And I remain in the mouth, renting games licenses is a practice that SHALL end. Far too often the companies took my money in the media, which they then decided to close. There are thousands of hundreds of ways in which a video game can be maintained even after the official server has been closed, companies must have a plan to offer the service they sold even after they decide to end it, otherwise it is in all respects a pyramid scheme, a rug pull, a theft. If I bought the game, the product SHALL be my own. Point.
